explicit_text: I was living at 602 South Windbrig Avenue in Springfield, Ohio when I was drafted into the Army. It was from the draft board in Springfield that I was sent to Fort Knox, Kentucky on the same day. Springfield was my home before and after my military service, and it’s where I returned after the war ended in October 1945.

explicit_text: I was drafted into the United States Army during World War II and served for almost four years. explicit_text: One of my most memorable experiences was during the Battle of the Bulge. Our truck company was trapped by German forces after they broke through the American lines near Normandy. We had to escape under heavy fire after a barrage was laid down, and I even had a bullet hit my rifle. Surviving that was a moment I celebrated with a bottle of German Schnapps.

explicit_text: I spent about two weeks stationed outside Paris, which was a wonderful break during my six to eight months overseas. While there, I visited many historical sites like the Louvre, the Tuileries Garden, the Palace of Versailles, the Eiffel Tower, and Notre-Dame Cathedral. Seeing these places was a highlight of my service and something I had only read about before.

explicit_text: During my service, I didn’t have much contact with family or correspondence because we were always on the move and communication was difficult.
